message:
bench: add interval_tree and quota_cost_ops criterion suites

Two new criterion harnesses for frankenterm-core modules that
previously had no perf coverage.  Both are registered in
crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml as [[bench]] entries with
harness=false (the convention already used by the rest of the bench
dir).

interval_tree_bench.rs covers the augmented interval tree in
frankenterm_core::interval_tree with explicit per-operation budgets:
insert < 200ns, point query < 500ns, overlap query < 1µs, remove
< 500ns, 10K inserts < 2ms, iter-all < 10µs for 1K elements.  These
numbers are the p50 targets we want CI to regress against; they are
also written into the BUDGETS table the other benches use so the
forbidden-token scan and the soak tooling can read them uniformly.

quota_cost_ops.rs mirrors the same shape for the quota/cost
accounting path — proactively adds perf coverage before we start
leaning on those ops inside the 10/50/200-pane tuning playbook, so
regressions would be caught before they reach the operator guidance.

files.patch:
@@ -445,6 +445,10 @@ harness = false
 name = "lfu_cache_bench"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "interval_tree_bench"
+harness = false
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "quota_cost_ops"
 harness = false
